response:
THE ART GALLERY SHOWCASES DIGITAL INSTALLATIONS RELYING ON SOPHISTICATED ALGORITHMS.
THE FIRST INSTALLATION USES A PROJECTION SYSTEM FOR DYNAMIC GEOMETRIC PATTERNS.
CLOSED LOOPS OCCUR WHEN A AND B ARE RATIONAL NUMBERS WITH A COMMON PERIOD.
THE PHASE SHIFT PHI DOES NOT AFFECT THE CLOSING CONDITION OF THE LOOP.
THE PERIOD T IS DETERMINED BY THE LEAST COMMON MULTIPLE OF THE TWO FREQUENCIES.
IF A AND B ARE INTEGERS, THE PERIOD IS TWO PI DIVIDED BY THEIR GREATEST COMMON DIVISOR.
THE SECOND INSTALLATION DISPLAYS A THREE DIMENSIONAL SURFACE USING AN IMPLICIT FUNCTION.
CRITICAL VALUES OF LAMBDA OCCUR AT POSITIVE TWO AND NEGATIVE ONE FOR TOPOLOGICAL CHANGES.
AT LAMBDA EQUALS NEGATIVE ONE, THE SURFACE DEGENERATES INTO A PLANE OR SINGULAR POINTS.
THE GOOD DESIGN EVOLVES AS THE PARAMETER LAMBDA MOVES BETWEEN THESE CRITICAL THRESHOLDS.
FOR LAMBDA GREATER THAN TWO, THE SURFACE BECOMES DISCONNECTED OR EMPTY IN REAL SPACE.
BETWEEN NEGATIVE ONE AND TWO, THE SURFACE FORMS A CONNECTED CLOSED MANIFOLD.
VISUAL APPEARANCE SHIFTS FROM SPHERICAL TO DISTORTED SHAPES AS LAMBDA INCREASES.
JOURNALISTS MUST NOTE THESE MATHEMATICAL TRANSITIONS IN THEIR REVIEW OF THE EXHIBITION.
THE INTERSECTION OF ART AND TECHNOLOGY IS CLEARLY DEMONSTRATED BY THESE TWO PIECES.
